rationale_for_baseline_tier: Uncomplicated superficial folliculitis is benign and self-managed with antiseptic washes, topical measures and removing mechanical/occlusive triggers (T0). A deep abscess or cellulitis routes to GP/ED (T4).

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: A spot becoming a large, deep, firm, very painful lump (boil/abscess), the redness joining into a hot spreading patch, a red streak up the leg, or a fever
escalation_edges.rationale: Furuncle/carbuncle needs drainage; cellulitis needs antibiotics ± ED.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If a spot turns into a large, deep, very painful lump, the redness spreads into a hot patch, you get a red streak up the leg, or you develop a fever — see a doctor / go to ED that day.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Reassure it is not a dangerous/MRSA infection; give a deep-infection safety-net.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Wash the area twice a day with an antibacterial wash, dab the topical treatment on the bigger spots, stop heavy lotions, throw away the razor and don't shave until healed. It should clear over about a week.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Self-care with telehealth/GP follow-up; GP/ED for a boil or spreading cellulitis
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: GP/ED for a large painful lump, spreading redness/streaking or fever.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Superficial shave/friction folliculitis of the lower legs; deep abscess/cellulitis screened negative; antiseptic wash + topical antibiotic + trigger removal; oral antibiotics withheld.

critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An evolving abscess or cellulitis is missed and spreads.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Not advising removal of the mechanical/occlusive triggers, so it persists/recurs.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: ED/ambulance or oral antibiotics for uncomplicated superficial folliculitis — unnecessary.
